skill.name: planner
skill.description: Create phased implementation plans for bugs, features, and technical tasks. The skill asks clarifying questions, researches context, drafts sprints, defines atomic tasks, and saves a Markdown plan.
skill.summary: Create phased implementation plans with sprints, atomic tasks, validation steps, and risk checks.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: The static external-command and weak-crypto findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, YAML text, and plan-template placeholders. No prompt injection, malicious command execution, credential access, or data exfiltration was found. The remaining risk is low because the skill writes a plan file and may retrieve external documentation through Context7.

low_findings.title: False Positive Markdown Backtick Detection
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 88 and 91 show Markdown inline filenames, and line 103 starts a Markdown template fence. No shell command, Ruby execution, or executable script is present. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The flagged tokens are inside documentation and a plan template, not executable code.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 3 and 6 are skill description text, and line 124 is a plan-template placeholder. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, encryption code, or credential handling is present. Confidence: 0.95. Confidence reasoning: The reviewed context contains natural language and placeholders only, so the weak-crypto pattern is not semantically valid.

low_findings.title: False Positive System Reconnaissance Detection
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 33 says clarification helps find the right documentation. It does not instruct the model to enumerate host data, collect system details, or run reconnaissance commands. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: The line is part of a planning workflow and has no command or data collection behavior.

low_findings.title: Plan File Creation Requires Workspace Care
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to save a Markdown plan file and show the saved path. This is expected behavior, but it can create files in the workspace and should avoid overwriting existing user files. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The file-save behavior is explicit in the skill text, but the exact write target depends on the user request and assistant implementation.

low_findings.title: Optional Documentation Retrieval Uses External Context
low_findings.description: The skill tells the assistant to use Context7 to fetch current official documentation when external dependencies apply. This is legitimate planning support, but it may involve network-backed documentation retrieval. Confidence: 0.80. Confidence reasoning: The instruction is explicit, but actual network behavior depends on whether Context7 is available in the execution environment.

content.value_statement: Complex implementation requests can become vague, risky, and hard to execute. This skill turns them into phased plans with sprints, atomic tasks, validation steps, and risk checks.
actual_capabilities: Investigates codebase architecture, patterns, dependencies, and related components before planning.
Asks clarification questions to reduce ambiguity before drafting the plan.
Fetches current official documentation through Context7 when external dependencies apply.
Creates phased sprints with demo and validation checkpoints.
Breaks each sprint into atomic, committable, independently testable tasks.
Saves the completed plan as a kebab-case Markdown file.
limitations: It creates plans only and does not implement the tasks.
Plan quality depends on available project context and user answers.
Documentation retrieval depends on Context7 availability.
Users should review generated filenames before saving in sensitive workspaces.

use_cases.title: Plan a Feature Build
use_cases.description: Turn a product request into staged engineering work with clear validation points.

use_cases.title: Break Down Technical Debt
use_cases.description: Convert a broad refactor into small tasks that can be assigned, tested, and merged.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Bug Fix
use_cases.description: Map investigation, fix scope, regression tests, and rollback steps before editing code.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Small Fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planner skill to create a plan for fixing this bug: [describe the bug]. Include investigation steps, files to inspect, tests, and validation.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a New Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planner skill to plan this feature: [feature request]. Ask clarifying questions first, then create phased sprints with atomic tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planner skill to break down this refactor: [scope]. Include dependencies, migration order, test strategy, rollback plan, and review checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Cross-Team Delivery
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planner skill to create an implementation plan for [initiative]. Include team handoffs, external documentation needs, risks, release validation, and follow-up questions.

output_examples.output: A Markdown plan with an overview, prerequisites, two or more sprints, atomic backend and test tasks, validation steps, risks, and rollback notes.

output_examples.input: Break down a database migration refactor.
output_examples.output: A phased plan that separates discovery, schema preparation, compatibility work, migration execution, verification, and fallback tasks.

output_examples.input: Plan a bug fix for failed webhook retries.
output_examples.output: A focused plan covering reproduction, root-cause analysis, code changes, retry tests, monitoring checks, and deployment validation.

best_practices: Provide the goal, current behavior, expected behavior, and relevant files when available.
Answer clarification questions before asking for the final plan.
Review the saved plan filename and scope before using it as delivery guidance.
anti_patterns: Using the skill to implement code instead of creating a plan.
Skipping clarification for ambiguous or high-risk work.
Treating generated estimates as commitments without team review.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. It creates an implementation plan and explicitly instructs the assistant not to implement the work.

faq.question: What format does the plan use?
faq.answer: The skill saves a Markdown file with an overview, sprints, tasks, validation steps, risks, and rollback notes.

faq.question: Does it ask questions before planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks clarification questions to reduce ambiguity before retrieving documentation or drafting tasks.

faq.question: Can it use current framework documentation?
faq.answer: Yes, when external dependencies apply and Context7 is available, it retrieves current official documentation.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: It does not contain malicious commands, but users should review any saved plan file and documentation requests.

faq.question: Who benefits most from this skill?
faq.answer: Developers, engineering leads, and product managers who need structured technical plans benefit most.
